scope: Narrow re-run of the 2 application specs only — measures whether conduct#43's 4000 → 8000 max_tokens raise unblocked markdown_subset on either Gemma. The 20 algorithm-spec cells from v4 are unchanged and not re-measured here.
notes: Headline: raising max_tokens 4000 → 8000 did NOT unblock either Gemma on these specs. e4b broke through the old ceiling (5377 / 5274 tokens vs the 4000 wall) but produced badly-malformed code on both specs. 12b timed out at 300s on markdown_subset and produced different-malformed code on kv_store_cli. Critically, v4's kv_store_cli/12b cleanly passed 5/5/5 at 1411 tokens — v5's same prompt/same model produced 2056 tokens of broken code. The 4000-token cap was acting as a forcing function; raising it gave the models room to ramble into duplicated logic and unclosed delimiters. Per-call stochasticity dominates these specs.

results.note: Output exceeded old 4000 cap (good — the cap raise worked at the wire level). Code is structurally broken: TWO parse_paragraph fns, TWO parse_inline_block fns, 8 compile errors.
Gen never completed. 12b cannot produce a markdown_subset response within the 300s Ollama timeout, regardless of token budget.
Output exceeded old 4000 cap. 'unclosed delimiter' — deeply nested match-within-match never balances braces.

Compile fail: 'non-exhaustive patterns: &_ not covered'. Source has 3 copies of the for-loop body (re-implementing then 'redoing internal logic'). Regression vs v4 where the same spec/model produced 1411 clean tokens and passed 5/5/5.
